title: ARTA JSON format
description: Schema for alternating real-time automata (ARTA) in LearnARTA JSON format. Transition targets use recursive positive Boolean syntax. Initial states accept either the legacy list-of-locations form or the same recursive positive Boolean syntax. Readers may also canonicalize legacy/original NRTA overlap encoding into deterministic ARTA transitions during import.

l.description: Finite set of locations (states) of the automaton.

sigma.description: Declared input alphabet. Unused symbols are allowed on input; writers may infer this set from transitions.

tran.description: Transition relation encoded as a map from transition IDs to 4-tuples [source, symbol, guard, target].

propertyNames.description: Transition ID written as a decimal string.

init.description: Initial state formula. For backward compatibility this may be a list of locations interpreted disjunctively, or a full positive Boolean formula.

target.description: Positive Boolean formula over locations. Strings denote atomic locations. Compound formulas use recursive 'and' / 'or' objects. Constants use {'const': true|false}.

oneOf.description: Atomic target: a single location identifier.

or.description: List of subtargets combined by logical OR.

and.description: List of subtargets combined by logical AND.

transition.description: A transition encoded as [source, symbol, guard, target].

prefixItems.description: Timing guard written as an interval string, such as '[2,3)', '(1,4)', or '[5,+)'.

$comment: This schema checks JSON structure only. Additional semantic checks, such as membership of referenced states in 'l', membership of symbols in 'sigma', validity of interval syntax, and pairwise disjointness of guards for each (source, symbol), must be implemented separately. LearnARTA readers may canonicalize legacy/original NRTA overlaps into deterministic ARTA before enforcing determinism.
